content.full_text:
December 9, 1977

A 77-427

Mr. Henry Giler
32 - 7th Place, #501
Long Beach, California 90802

Dear Mr. Giler:

Thank you for your letter of November 26, 1977. The term
"contribution" is defined in the enclosed PPPC Regulation 18215.
The answer to both questions is that both of the individuals
involved in each of the transactions are contributors. The
difference is one individual has made a non-monetary contribution because of the "donation" of the item and, the individual
who purchases the donated item has made a monetary contribution.
Both individuals must be listed as contributors on the campaign
statement if each transaction totals $50 or more.

I have enclosed additional material that will be of assistance
to you.

Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.

Sincerely,


Mary Ann Kvasager
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division

Enclosure

MAK:bw

Henry Giler
22 - 7th Place, #501
Long Beach, California 90802

November 26, 1977

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, Ca. 95814

Dear Sir:

I am going to be treasurer for Edith Pollach, who is going to
run for City Council in The First District of Long Beach. The
only material I have is a January, 1976 information manual.

Would you please send me whatever you think I would need including
the required forms.

Going through your manual, I find at least two questions unanswered.
What happens when one party donates an art object, and it is sold
at a party? Who made the donation- the one who contributed the
article or the one who buys it?

Another question is along the same line. A local theatre owner
is willing to refund to our campaign one half of the admission
price of those persons who attend his theatre after being
referred by us. Again, who is the contributor?

Sincerely,

Henry Giler

sections.question: What happens when one party donates an art object, and it is sold at a party? Who made the donation- the one who contributed the article or the one who buys it? Another question is along the same line. A local theatre owner is willing to refund to our campaign one half of the admission price of those persons who attend his theatre after being referred by us. Again, who is the contributor?
sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks clarification on campaign contribution attribution rules, specifically regarding donated art objects sold at fundraising events and theatre admission price refunds from a local theatre owner.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/letter from a member of the public to the FPPC, not an FPPC response letter. No CONCLUSION section exists. Document is dated November 26, 1917 (likely a typo for 1977 based on document ID year). Some text appears corrupted or illegible at the top of the document.

Again, who is the contributor?
